name: Install-PackageProvider

options.name: -AllVersions
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet installs all available versions of the package provider. By default, `Install-PackageProvider` only returns the highest available version.

options.name: -Credential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to install package providers.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet forces all actions with this cmdlet that can be forced. Currently, this means the Force parameter acts the same as the ForceBootstrap parameter.

options.name: -ForceBootstrap
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet automatically installs the package provider.

options.name: -InputObject
options.description: Specifies a SoftwareIdentity object. Use the `Find-PackageProvider` cmdlet to obtain a SoftwareIdentity object to pipe into `Install-PackageProvider`.

options.name: -MaximumVersion
options.description: Specifies the maximum allowed version of the package provider that you want to install. If you do not add this parameter, `Install-PackageProvider` installs the highest available version of the provider.

options.name: -MinimumVersion
options.description: Specifies the minimum allowed version of the package provider that you want to install. If you do not add this parameter, `Install-PackageProvider` installs the highest available version of the package that also satisfies any requirement specified by the MaximumVersion parameter.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies one or more package provider module names. Separate multiple package names with commas. Wildcard characters are not supported.

options.name: -Proxy
options.description: Specifies a proxy server for the request, rather than connecting directly to the Internet resource.

options.name: -ProxyCredential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to use the proxy server that is specified by the Proxy parameter.

options.name: -RequiredVersion
options.description: Specifies the exact allowed version of the package provider that you want to install. If you do not add this parameter, `Install-PackageProvider` installs the highest available version of the provider that also satisfies any maximum version specified by the MaximumVersion parameter.

options.name: -Scope
options.description: Specifies the installation scope of the provider. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

options.name: -Source
options.description: Specifies one or more package sources. Use the `Get-PackageSource` cmdlet to get a list of available package sources.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

description: Installs one or more Package Management package providers.
